---
title: "APTC 2026 Survey Results"
subtitle: "Association of Psychology Training Clinics"
author: "Jacqueline Hersh, Ph.D.<br>Ryan L. Farmer, Ph.D.<br>Richard LeBeau, Ph.D."
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    logo: ""
    footer: "APTC 2026 Annual Survey"
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    highlight-style: github
    code-fold: true
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(gt)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# APTC Colors
aptc_navy  <- "#252b3b"
aptc_coral <- "#ff6544"
aptc_teal  <- "#31a8c0"

aptc_palette <- c(
 aptc_coral, "#ff8f77", "#e24f33",
 aptc_teal, "#5bc0d4", "#1f7f92",
 aptc_navy, "#454d5f", "#161b29"
)

# Tenure-track helper functions (defined once, used throughout)
nz <- function(x) ifelse(is.na(x), "", as.character(x))
is_tt <- function(x) {
  str_detect(nz(x), regex("tenure[- ]track|tenured(?!.*non)", ignore_case = TRUE)) &
  !str_detect(nz(x), regex("non.?tenure", ignore_case = TRUE))
}
is_ntt <- function(x) {
  str_detect(nz(x), regex("non.?tenure|clinical.?track|instructor|lecturer|visiting",
                           ignore_case = TRUE))
}

# Load and clean data (same as analysis)
dat <- read_excel("dat.xlsx")
dat <- dat[-1, ]

dat <- dat %>%
 mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ na_if(., -99))) %>%
 mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(., "-99"))) %>%
 mutate(
   across(
     matches("Binary$"),
     ~ case_when(
       str_detect(., regex("^yes$", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Yes",
       str_detect(., regex("^no$",  ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "No",
       TRUE ~ NA_character_
     )
   )
 ) %>%
 mutate(
   Progress = as.numeric(Progress),
   GrossSalary = as.numeric(GrossSalary),
   FTESalary = as.numeric(FTESalary),
   RoleDuration = as.numeric(RoleDuration),
   ClinicDuration = as.numeric(ClinicDuration),
   ClinicAge = as.numeric(ClinicAge),
   HoursWorkClinic = as.numeric(HoursWorkClinic),
   CoursesTaught = as.numeric(CoursesTaught),
   ClinicTotalTrainees = as.numeric(ClinicTotalTrainees),
   ClinicClientsCount = as.numeric(ClinicClientsCount),
   NumRooms = as.numeric(NumRooms),
   MaxTraineeCount = as.numeric(MaxTraineeCount),
   ClinicRevenue = as.numeric(ClinicRevenue),
   Burnout_1 = as.numeric(Burnout_1),
   Burnout_2 = as.numeric(Burnout_2),
   Burnout_3 = as.numeric(Burnout_3),
   Burnout_4 = as.numeric(Burnout_4),
   Burnout_5 = as.numeric(Burnout_5),
   FTESupportStaff = as.numeric(FTESupportStaff),
   ClinicSupportStaff = as.numeric(ClinicSupportStaff),
   FTEGradStaff = as.numeric(FTEGradStaff),
   GradStaff = as.numeric(GradStaff),
   UgradStaff = as.numeric(UgradStaff),
   PostdocCount = as.numeric(PostdocCount),
   ClinicHours = as.numeric(ClinicHours),
   MaxTraineesTherapy = as.numeric(MaxTraineesTherapy),
   MaxTraineesAssmnt = as.numeric(MaxTraineesAssmnt),
   TxLength = as.numeric(TxLength),
   MaxTxCasesAllow = as.numeric(MaxTxCasesAllow),
   MaxAxCasesAllow = as.numeric(MaxAxCasesAllow)
 )

# Clean ClinSqFt as categorical
dat <- dat %>%
 mutate(
   ClinSqFt = case_when(
     ClinSqFt == "-99" ~ NA_character_,
     TRUE ~ ClinSqFt
   ),
   ClinSqFt = factor(ClinSqFt, levels = c(
     "Under 500 sq ft", "500–999 sq ft", "1,000–1,999 sq ft",
     "2,000–4,999 sq ft", "5,000 sq ft or more"
   ))
 )

# Rank grouping
dat <- dat %>%
 mutate(
   # Coarse rank — used for charts that do not split by TT status
   RankGrp = case_when(
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("assistant", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Assistant Professor",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("associate", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Associate Professor",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("^full|full professor", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Full Professor",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("lecturer|instructor", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Lecturer",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("staff|n/a|not applicable", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Staff (N/A)",
     !is.na(Rank) ~ "Other (specified)",
     TRUE ~ "Unspecified Rank"
   ),
   # Detailed rank — separates Clinical/NTT from TT at each level
   RankGrpDetailed = case_when(
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("assistant", ignore_case = TRUE)) & is_tt(PositionType)  ~ "TT Assistant Professor",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("assistant", ignore_case = TRUE)) & is_ntt(PositionType) ~ "Clinical/NTT Assistant Professor",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("assistant", ignore_case = TRUE))                        ~ "Assistant Professor (other)",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("associate", ignore_case = TRUE)) & is_tt(PositionType)  ~ "TT Associate Professor",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("associate", ignore_case = TRUE)) & is_ntt(PositionType) ~ "Clinical/NTT Associate Professor",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("associate", ignore_case = TRUE))                        ~ "Associate Professor (other)",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("^full|full professor", ignore_case = TRUE))             ~ "Full Professor",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("lecturer|instructor", ignore_case = TRUE))              ~ "Lecturer",
     str_detect(nz(Rank), regex("staff|n/a|not applicable", ignore_case = TRUE))        ~ "Staff (N/A)",
     !is.na(Rank) ~ "Other (specified)",
     TRUE ~ "Unspecified Rank"
   )
 )
```

# Sample Overview {background-color="#252b3b"}

::: notes
Welcome to the 2026 APTC Annual Survey results. This presentation covers key findings from our membership survey, including sample demographics, salary data, clinic operations, and director wellbeing.
:::

## Who Responded?

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
mem_status <- dat %>%
 count(MemStatus) %>%
 mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))

ggplot(mem_status %>% filter(!is.na(MemStatus)), 
      aes(x = reorder(MemStatus, n), y = n, fill = MemStatus)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
 coord_flip() +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 140)) +
 labs(title = "Membership Status", x = NULL, y = "Count") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
### Sample Highlights

-   **N = `r nrow(dat)`** total respondents
-   **`r sum(dat$MemStatus == "Member", na.rm = TRUE)`** full members (`r round(100*sum(dat$MemStatus == "Member", na.rm = TRUE)/nrow(dat), 1)`%)
-   **`r sum(dat$MemStatus == "Associate Member", na.rm = TRUE)`** associate members

::: callout-note
Throughout this presentation, n's vary because not everyone answered every question.
:::
::::
::::::

::: notes
Our sample includes 120 respondents, with 92% being full APTC members. Sample sizes vary across analyses due to item-level missingness.
:::

## Professional Background

```{r}
prof_deg <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ProfDeg)) %>%
 count(ProfDeg) %>%
 mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
 arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(prof_deg, aes(x = reorder(ProfDeg, n), y = Percentage, fill = ProfDeg)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
 coord_flip() +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
 labs(title = "Professional Degree", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
The vast majority of clinic directors hold doctoral degrees in psychology, with Ph.D. being the most common credential.
:::

## Degree Area

```{r}
deg_area <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(DegArea)) %>%
  count(DegArea) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(deg_area, aes(x = reorder(DegArea, n), y = Percentage, fill = DegArea)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(title = "Graduate Degree Areas", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Most clinic directors specialize in clinical psychology, though counseling and school psychology are also well-represented.
:::

## Experience in Role

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
role_stats <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(RoleDuration)) %>%
 summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(RoleDuration), SD = sd(RoleDuration),
           Median = median(RoleDuration), Min = min(RoleDuration), Max = max(RoleDuration))

dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(RoleDuration)) %>%
 ggplot(aes(x = RoleDuration)) +
 geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = aptc_teal, color = "white", alpha = 0.9) +
 geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(RoleDuration)), color = aptc_coral, 
            linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
 labs(title = "Years in Current Role",
      subtitle = paste0("Median = ", role_stats$Median, " years"),
      x = "Years", y = "Count") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Role Duration

| Statistic | Value                                         |
|-----------|-----------------------------------------------|
| N         | `r role_stats$N`                              |
| Mean      | `r round(role_stats$Mean, 1)` years           |
| Median    | `r role_stats$Median` years                   |
| Range     | `r role_stats$Min` – `r role_stats$Max` years |
:::
:::::

::: notes
Directors have a median of X years in their current role, with a wide range reflecting both new directors and long-tenured leaders.
:::

# Position Characteristics {background-color="#252b3b"}

## Academic Rank

```{r}
rank_summary <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(RankGrp)) %>%
 count(RankGrp) %>%
 mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
 arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(rank_summary, aes(x = reorder(RankGrp, n), y = Percentage, fill = RankGrp)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
 coord_flip() +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 50)) +
 labs(title = "Academic Rank Distribution", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Associate and Assistant Professor ranks are most common among clinic directors. A notable proportion hold staff positions without faculty rank.
:::

## Academic Rank by Tenure Status

```{r}
rank_tenure <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Rank), !is.na(PositionType)) %>%
  mutate(
    Rank_clean = case_when(
      str_detect(Rank, regex("assistant", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Assistant",
      str_detect(Rank, regex("associate", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Associate",
      str_detect(Rank, regex("^full|full professor", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Full",
      str_detect(Rank, regex("lecturer|instructor", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Lecturer",
      str_detect(Rank, regex("staff|n/a", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Staff/N.A.",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    TenureStatus = case_when(
      is_tt(PositionType) ~ "TT/Tenured",
      is_ntt(PositionType) ~ "NTT",
      TRUE ~ "Other/Admin"
    )
  )

tenure_table <- rank_tenure %>%
  count(Rank_clean, TenureStatus) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = TenureStatus, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)

tenure_table %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Rank", names(tenure_table)[-1]), caption = "Academic Rank by Tenure Track Status") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), font_size = 18)
```

::: notes
The majority of clinic directors, across all ranks, hold non-tenure-track positions. Tenure-track positions are relatively rare for this role.
:::

## Who Do We Report To?

```{r}
sup_summary <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Supervisor)) %>%
  count(Supervisor) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(sup_summary, aes(x = reorder(Supervisor, n), y = Percentage, fill = Supervisor)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 60)) +
  labs(title = "Who Do Directors Report To?", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Most clinic directors report to a department chair or program director. The reporting structure reflects the academic nature of most training clinics.
:::

## Contract Characteristics

```{r}
contract_summary <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ContractLength)) %>%
 count(ContractLength) %>%
 mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
 arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(contract_summary, aes(x = reorder(ContractLength, n), y = Percentage, fill = ContractLength)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
 coord_flip() +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 50)) +
 labs(title = "Contract Length", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Contract lengths vary considerably, with yearly contracts and tenured/permanent positions being most common.
:::

## Contract Type (Months)

```{r}
contract_months <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(ContractMonths)) %>%
  count(ContractMonths) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(contract_months, aes(x = reorder(ContractMonths, n), y = Percentage, fill = ContractMonths)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 90)) +
  labs(title = "Contract Type (Months)", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
The vast majority of clinic directors hold 12-month contracts, which reflects the administrative and service demands of the role that extend beyond the traditional academic year.
:::

# Salary Analysis {background-color="#252b3b"}

## Overall Salary Distribution

```{r}
salary_stats <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(GrossSalary)) %>%
 summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(GrossSalary), SD = sd(GrossSalary),
           Median = median(GrossSalary), Q1 = quantile(GrossSalary, 0.25),
           Q3 = quantile(GrossSalary, 0.75))

dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(GrossSalary)) %>%
 ggplot(aes(x = GrossSalary)) +
 geom_histogram(binwidth = 10000, fill = aptc_teal, color = "white", alpha = 0.9) +
 geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(GrossSalary)), color = aptc_coral, 
            linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
 scale_x_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
 labs(title = "Gross Salary Distribution",
      subtitle = paste0("Median = ", dollar(salary_stats$Median), " | Mean = ", dollar(round(salary_stats$Mean))),
      x = "Gross Salary", y = "Count") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
The median gross salary is approximately \$104,000, with a mean of \$106,000. There is considerable variation across positions.
:::

## Salary by Academic Rank

```{r}
dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(GrossSalary), !is.na(RankGrpDetailed),
        RankGrpDetailed != "Unspecified Rank") %>%
 ggplot(aes(x = reorder(RankGrpDetailed, GrossSalary, median),
            y = GrossSalary, fill = RankGrpDetailed)) +
 geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3, size = 2) +
 coord_flip() +
 scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
 scale_fill_manual(values = rep(aptc_palette, length.out = 9)) +
 labs(title = "Gross Salary by Academic Rank", x = NULL, y = "Gross Salary") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
As expected, salary increases with rank progression. Full professors have the highest median salary, followed by associate and assistant professors.
:::

## Salary Summary Table

```{r}
salary_by_rank <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(GrossSalary), !is.na(RankGrpDetailed),
        RankGrpDetailed != "Unspecified Rank") %>%
 group_by(RankGrpDetailed) %>%
 summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(GrossSalary), Median = median(GrossSalary),
           SD = sd(GrossSalary), .groups = "drop") %>%
 filter(N >= 3) %>%
 arrange(desc(Median))

salary_by_rank %>%
 mutate(
   Mean   = dollar(round(Mean)),
   Median = dollar(round(Median)),
   SD     = ifelse(N < 5, "\u2014", dollar(round(SD)))
 ) %>%
 kable(col.names = c("Rank", "N", "Mean", "Median", "SD"),
       caption = "Cells with N < 3 suppressed for privacy") %>%
 kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), font_size = 18)
```

::: notes
This table separates Clinical/NTT from TT positions at each rank level, matching the breakdown shared after the 2024 conference. Cells with fewer than 3 respondents are suppressed. SD is shown as — for cells with N \< 5.
:::

## Salary by Contract Length

```{r}
dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(GrossSalary), !is.na(ContractLength)) %>%
 ggplot(aes(x = reorder(ContractLength, GrossSalary, median), y = GrossSalary, fill = ContractLength)) +
 geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3, size = 2) +
 coord_flip() +
 scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 labs(title = "Salary by Contract Length", x = NULL, y = "Gross Salary") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Tenured and permanent positions tend to have higher salaries than yearly or multi-year contracts.
:::

## Salary by Position Type

```{r}
salary_postype <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(GrossSalary), !is.na(PositionType)) %>%
  mutate(
    PosGroup = case_when(
      is_tt(PositionType)  ~ "Tenure-Track/Tenured",
      is_ntt(PositionType) ~ "Non-Tenure-Track",
      str_detect(nz(PositionType), regex("admin|staff|director", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Admin/Staff",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(PosGroup != "Other") %>%
  group_by(PosGroup) %>%
  summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(GrossSalary), Median = median(GrossSalary), SD = sd(GrossSalary), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(Median))

salary_postype %>%
  mutate(Mean = dollar(round(Mean)), Median = dollar(round(Median)), SD = dollar(round(SD))) %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Position Type", "N", "Mean", "Median", "SD"),
        caption = "Salary by Position Type") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), font_size = 20)
```

::: notes
Admin/staff positions show the highest salaries, followed by tenure-track and then non-tenure-track positions. These differences reflect both rank and the nature of the roles.
:::

## Professional Development Funding

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
pd_dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    PD_access = case_when(
      grepl("^Yes", PDFunds) ~ 1L,
      PDFunds == "No" ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    PD_amount = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(PDFunds_2_TEXT)),
    PD_amount = ifelse(PD_amount == -99, NA, PD_amount)
  )

pd_access_summary <- pd_dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(PD_access)) %>%
  mutate(AccessLabel = case_when(
    PD_access == 1L ~ "Yes, consistently",
    PD_access == 0L ~ "No",
    TRUE ~ "Sometimes"
  )) %>%
  count(AccessLabel) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))

ggplot(pd_access_summary, aes(x = reorder(AccessLabel, Percentage), y = Percentage, fill = AccessLabel)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 90)) +
  labs(title = "Professional Development Funding Access", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
pd_amount_stats <- pd_dat %>%
  filter(PD_access == 1L, !is.na(PD_amount)) %>%
  summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(PD_amount), SD = sd(PD_amount),
            Median = median(PD_amount), Min = min(PD_amount), Max = max(PD_amount))
```

### PD Funding Summary

| Statistic | Value |
|----|----|
| N | `r pd_amount_stats$N` |
| Mean | `r dollar(round(pd_amount_stats$Mean))` |
| Median | `r dollar(round(pd_amount_stats$Median))` |
| Range | `r dollar(pd_amount_stats$Min)` – `r dollar(pd_amount_stats$Max)` |

::: callout-note
Among those with consistent PD access.
:::
::::
::::::

::: notes
Most directors receive some form of professional development funding. The median amount for those with consistent access provides important context for benchmarking.
:::

## Salary Funding Sources

```{r}
funding_labels <- c(
  "FundingSrc_1" = "Department/University",
  "FundingSrc_2" = "College",
  "FundingSrc_3" = "Clinic Fees",
  "FundingSrc_4" = "Individual Client Fees",
  "FundingSrc_5" = "Grants",
  "FundingSrc_6" = "Community Contracts",
  "FundingSrc_7" = "Other"
)

funding_summary <- dat %>%
  select(starts_with("FundingSrc_")) %>%
  select(-contains("TEXT")) %>%
  select(any_of(names(funding_labels))) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(!is.na(.) & . != "0" & . != "", na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Count") %>%
  mutate(Source = funding_labels[Variable]) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Source)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * Count / nrow(dat), 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(Count))

ggplot(funding_summary, aes(x = reorder(Source, Count), y = Count, fill = Source)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Count, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  scale_x_discrete() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(funding_summary$Count) * 1.3)) +
  labs(title = "Salary Funding Sources (Select All That Apply)", 
       subtitle = "Note: % reflects proportion of all respondents endorsing each source",
       x = NULL, y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Department and university funds are by far the most common salary source. Only a minority receive salary funding from clinic fees or grants, highlighting the academic rather than fee-for-service structure of most training clinics.
:::

# Clinic Operations {background-color="#252b3b"}

## Clinic Age & Training Model

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
age_stats <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinicAge), ClinicAge >= 0) %>%
 summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(ClinicAge), Median = median(ClinicAge))

dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinicAge), ClinicAge >= 0) %>%
 ggplot(aes(x = ClinicAge)) +
 geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = aptc_teal, color = "white", alpha = 0.9) +
 geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(ClinicAge)), color = aptc_coral, 
            linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
 labs(title = "Clinic Age (Years)",
      subtitle = paste0("Median = ", age_stats$Median, " years"),
      x = "Years", y = "Count") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
model_summary <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinicTrainingModel)) %>%
 count(ClinicTrainingModel) %>%
 mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
 arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(model_summary, aes(x = reorder(ClinicTrainingModel, n), y = Percentage, fill = ClinicTrainingModel)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, "\n(", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 4) +
 coord_flip() +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 80)) +
 labs(title = "Training Model", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::
:::::

::: notes
Most clinics have been operating for decades. The scientist-practitioner model remains the most common training approach.
:::

## Clinic Size: Clients & Trainees

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
client_stats <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinicClientsCount), ClinicClientsCount > 0) %>%
 summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(ClinicClientsCount), Median = median(ClinicClientsCount))

dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinicClientsCount), ClinicClientsCount > 0, ClinicClientsCount < 500) %>%
 ggplot(aes(x = ClinicClientsCount)) +
 geom_histogram(binwidth = 25, fill = aptc_coral, color = "white", alpha = 0.9) +
 geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(ClinicClientsCount)), color = aptc_navy, 
            linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
 labs(title = "Clients per Year",
      subtitle = paste0("Median = ", round(client_stats$Median)),
      x = "Number of Clients", y = "Count") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
trainee_stats <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinicTotalTrainees), ClinicTotalTrainees > 0) %>%
 summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(ClinicTotalTrainees), Median = median(ClinicTotalTrainees))

dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinicTotalTrainees), ClinicTotalTrainees > 0) %>%
 ggplot(aes(x = ClinicTotalTrainees)) +
 geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = aptc_teal, color = "white", alpha = 0.9) +
 geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(ClinicTotalTrainees)), color = aptc_coral, 
            linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
 labs(title = "Trainees per Year",
      subtitle = paste0("Median = ", round(trainee_stats$Median)),
      x = "Number of Trainees", y = "Count") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::
:::::

::: notes
Clinic sizes vary considerably. The median clinic serves around 110 clients and trains approximately 15 students per year.
:::

## Clinic Space

```{r}
sqft_summary <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinSqFt)) %>%
 count(ClinSqFt) %>%
 mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))

ggplot(sqft_summary, aes(x = ClinSqFt, y = n, fill = ClinSqFt)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, "\n(", Percentage, "%)")), vjust = -0.2, size = 5) +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(sqft_summary$n) * 1.3)) +
 labs(title = "Clinic Space Distribution", 
      subtitle = paste0("N = ", sum(sqft_summary$n)),
      x = NULL, y = "Count") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy),
       axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

::: notes
Most clinics fall in the 1,000 to 5,000 square foot range, with the most common category being 2,000-4,999 square feet.
:::

## Staff Composition

```{r}
staff_presence <- dat %>%
 summarise(
   `Has Support Staff`   = mean(!is.na(FTESupportStaff) & FTESupportStaff > 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
   `Has Grad Staff`      = mean(!is.na(FTEGradStaff) & FTEGradStaff > 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
   `Has Undergrad Staff` = mean(!is.na(HoursUgradStaff) & HoursUgradStaff > 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
   `Has Postdocs`        = mean(!is.na(PostdocCount) & PostdocCount > 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
 ) %>%
 pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Staff Type", values_to = "Percentage")

ggplot(staff_presence, aes(x = reorder(`Staff Type`, Percentage), y = Percentage, fill = `Staff Type`)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Percentage, 0), "%")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
 coord_flip() +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 labs(title = "Percentage of Clinics with Various Staff Types", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
About half of clinics report having support staff (57%) or graduate staff (50%). 
Fewer have undergraduate staff (29%), and postdoctoral trainees remain rare (7%).
:::

## Director Responsibilities

```{r}
resp_labels <- c(
  "Responsibilities_1"  = "Administration",
  "Responsibilities_2"  = "Teaching",
  "Responsibilities_3"  = "Supervision",
  "Responsibilities_4"  = "Direct Patient Services",
  "Responsibilities_5"  = "Research",
  "Responsibilities_6"  = "University/Department Service",
  "Responsibilities_7"  = "Community/Professional Service",
  "Responsibilities_8"  = "External Practicum Placements",
  "Responsibilities_10" = "Official Mentoring",
  "Responsibilities_11" = "Unofficial Mentoring",
  "Responsibilities_13" = "Crisis Response",
  "Responsibilities_14" = "Consulting",
  "Responsibilities_15" = "Outreach"
)

resp_summary <- dat %>%
  select(starts_with("Responsibilities_")) %>%
  select(-contains("TEXT")) %>%
  select(any_of(names(resp_labels))) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(!is.na(.) & . != "0" & . != "", na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Count") %>%
  mutate(Responsibility = resp_labels[Variable]) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Responsibility)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * Count / nrow(dat), 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(Count))

ggplot(resp_summary, aes(x = reorder(Responsibility, Count), y = Count, fill = Responsibility)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Count, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 4.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = rep(aptc_palette, length.out = nrow(resp_summary))) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(resp_summary$Count) * 1.3)) +
  labs(title = "Director Responsibilities (Select All That Apply)", x = NULL, y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Administration and supervision are near-universal responsibilities. Direct patient services, teaching, and research are also common, underscoring the multi-faceted nature of the clinic director role.
:::

## Clinic Hours

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
hours_stats <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(ClinicHours), ClinicHours > 0, ClinicHours <= 80) %>%
  summarise(N = n(), Mean = round(mean(ClinicHours), 1), Median = median(ClinicHours),
            Min = min(ClinicHours), Max = max(ClinicHours))

dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(ClinicHours), ClinicHours > 0, ClinicHours <= 80) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ClinicHours)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = aptc_teal, color = "white", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(ClinicHours)), color = aptc_coral,
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Weekly Clinic Hours",
       subtitle = paste0("Median = ", hours_stats$Median, " hrs | Mean = ", hours_stats$Mean, " hrs"),
       x = "Hours per Week", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
### Hours Summary

| Statistic | Value                                         |
|-----------|-----------------------------------------------|
| N         | `r hours_stats$N`                             |
| Mean      | `r hours_stats$Mean` hrs/week                 |
| Median    | `r hours_stats$Median` hrs/week               |
| Range     | `r hours_stats$Min` – `r hours_stats$Max` hrs |

::: callout-note
Hours reflect clinic operating hours, not director work hours.
:::
::::
::::::

::: notes
Most clinics operate approximately 40 hours per week. Some extend evening or weekend hours to serve student and community needs.
:::

## Clinical Supervisors & Caseloads

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
supervisor_stats <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(MaxTraineeCount), MaxTraineeCount > 0) %>%
  summarise(N = n(), Mean = round(mean(MaxTraineeCount), 1), 
            Median = median(MaxTraineeCount),
            Min = min(MaxTraineeCount), Max = max(MaxTraineeCount))

dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(MaxTraineeCount), MaxTraineeCount > 0, MaxTraineeCount <= 50) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = MaxTraineeCount)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = aptc_coral, color = "white", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(MaxTraineeCount)), color = aptc_navy,
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Max Trainees per Supervisor",
       subtitle = paste0("Median = ", supervisor_stats$Median),
       x = "Number of Trainees", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Student Caseload

```{r}
caseload_data <- dat %>%
  summarise(
    `Max Therapy Trainees` = paste0(round(mean(MaxTraineesTherapy, na.rm=TRUE), 1), 
                                    " (Mdn = ", median(MaxTraineesTherapy, na.rm=TRUE), ")"),
    `Max Assessment Trainees` = paste0(round(mean(MaxTraineesAssmnt, na.rm=TRUE), 1), 
                                       " (Mdn = ", median(MaxTraineesAssmnt, na.rm=TRUE), ")"),
    `Max Therapy Cases` = paste0(round(mean(MaxTxCasesAllow, na.rm=TRUE), 1), 
                                 " (Mdn = ", median(MaxTxCasesAllow, na.rm=TRUE), ")"),
    `Max Assessment Cases` = paste0(round(mean(MaxAxCasesAllow, na.rm=TRUE), 1), 
                                    " (Mdn = ", median(MaxAxCasesAllow, na.rm=TRUE), ")")
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value")

caseload_data %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Metric", "Mean (Median)"),
        caption = "Student Caseload & Supervisor Team Size") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), font_size = 18)
```
:::
:::::

::: notes
These metrics help clinics benchmark their caseload expectations against peers. High trainee-to-supervisor ratios can affect training quality and supervisor wellbeing.
:::

# Services & Fees {background-color="#252b3b"}

## Services Offered

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
therapy_pct <- sum(dat$ClinicTherapyBinary == "Yes", na.rm = TRUE) / 
              sum(!is.na(dat$ClinicTherapyBinary)) * 100
assess_pct <- sum(dat$ClinicAssmntBinary == "Yes", na.rm = TRUE) / 
             sum(!is.na(dat$ClinicAssmntBinary)) * 100

services <- tibble(
 Service = c("Therapy", "Assessment"),
 Percentage = c(therapy_pct, assess_pct)
)

ggplot(services, aes(x = Service, y = Percentage, fill = Service)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Percentage, 0), "%")), vjust = -0.5, size = 6) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 110)) +
 scale_fill_manual(values = c(aptc_teal, aptc_coral)) +
 labs(title = "Services Offered", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
### Service Highlights

-   **`r round(therapy_pct)`%** offer therapy
-   **`r round(assess_pct)`%** offer assessment
-   Most clinics offer both services

```{r}
sliding <- sum(dat$ClinicSlidingScale == "Yes", na.rm = TRUE) / 
          sum(!is.na(dat$ClinicSlidingScale)) * 100
```

::: callout-tip
**`r round(sliding)`%** of clinics use sliding scale fees
:::
::::
::::::

::: notes
Nearly all clinics offer therapy services, with assessment also being very common. The majority use sliding scale fee structures.
:::

## Clinic Revenue

```{r}
revenue_stats <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinicRevenue), ClinicRevenue > 0) %>%
 summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(ClinicRevenue), Median = median(ClinicRevenue),
           Min = min(ClinicRevenue), Max = max(ClinicRevenue))

dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(ClinicRevenue), ClinicRevenue > 0, ClinicRevenue < 500000) %>%
 ggplot(aes(x = ClinicRevenue)) +
 geom_histogram(binwidth = 25000, fill = aptc_teal, color = "white", alpha = 0.9) +
 geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(ClinicRevenue)), color = aptc_coral, 
            linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
 scale_x_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
 labs(title = "Annual Clinic Revenue",
      subtitle = paste0("Median = ", dollar(revenue_stats$Median), " (N = ", revenue_stats$N, ")"),
      x = "Revenue", y = "Count") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Median clinic revenue is approximately \$44,000, though there is substantial variation. Some clinics generate over \$500,000 annually.
:::

## Fee Structures

```{r}
therapy_fee_vars <- c("ClinicTherapyFees_1", "ClinicTherapyFees_2", "ClinicTherapyFees_3")
assmnt_fee_vars  <- c("ClinicAssmntFees_1", "ClinicAssmntFees_2", "ClinicAssmntFees_3")

fee_summary <- function(vars, service_name) {
  dat %>%
    select(all_of(vars)) %>%
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Value), Value > 0, Value < 10000) %>%
    mutate(FeeType = case_when(
      str_detect(Variable, "_1$") ~ "Minimum",
      str_detect(Variable, "_2$") ~ "Maximum",
      str_detect(Variable, "_3$") ~ "Typical"
    )) %>%
    group_by(FeeType) %>%
    summarise(N = n(), Mean = round(mean(Value), 0), Median = round(median(Value), 0), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(Service = service_name)
}

therapy_fees <- fee_summary(therapy_fee_vars, "Therapy")
assmnt_fees  <- fee_summary(assmnt_fee_vars, "Assessment")

bind_rows(therapy_fees, assmnt_fees) %>%
  select(Service, FeeType, N, Mean, Median) %>%
  mutate(Mean = dollar(Mean), Median = dollar(Median)) %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Service", "Fee Type", "N", "Mean", "Median"),
        caption = "Standard Fee Structures") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), font_size = 18) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```

::: notes
Assessment fees are considerably higher than therapy fees, reflecting the time and materials involved. Most clinics use sliding scale structures, making these maximums rarely charged.
:::

## Insurance Billing

```{r}
if ("ClinicBillBinary" %in% names(dat)) {
  insur_summary <- dat %>%
    filter(!is.na(ClinicBillBinary)) %>%
    count(ClinicBillBinary) %>%
    mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))

  ggplot(insur_summary, aes(x = ClinicBillBinary, y = Percentage, fill = ClinicBillBinary)) +
    geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), vjust = -0.3, size = 6) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
    labs(title = "Insurance Billing", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
} else {
  cat("Insurance billing variable not found in this dataset.\n")
}
```

::: notes
Very few training clinics bill insurance, keeping administrative overhead low while maintaining accessible services through sliding scale fees.
:::

# Technology {background-color="#252b3b"}

## EMR & Telehealth Adoption

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
emr_summary <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(EMRbinary)) %>%
 count(EMRbinary) %>%
 mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))

ggplot(emr_summary, aes(x = EMRbinary, y = Percentage, fill = EMRbinary)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, "\n(", Percentage, "%)")), vjust = -0.3, size = 5) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
 scale_fill_manual(values = c("Yes" = aptc_teal, "No" = aptc_coral)) +
 labs(title = "EMR Usage", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
telehealth_summary <- dat %>%
 filter(!is.na(Telehealth)) %>%
 count(Telehealth) %>%
 mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))

ggplot(telehealth_summary, aes(x = Telehealth, y = Percentage, fill = Telehealth)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, "\n(", Percentage, "%)")), vjust = -0.3, size = 5) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 labs(title = "Telehealth Services", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::
:::::

::: notes
Most clinics have adopted electronic medical records and offer telehealth services, reflecting the ongoing digital transformation in healthcare training.
:::

## Artificial Intelligence in Clinics

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
ai_clindir <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(AIUseClinDir)) %>%
  count(AIUseClinDir) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))

ggplot(ai_clindir, aes(x = AIUseClinDir, y = Percentage, fill = AIUseClinDir)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), vjust = -0.3, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  labs(title = "Director AI Use", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
ai_students <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(AIUseStudentsAllowed)) %>%
  count(AIUseStudentsAllowed) %>%
  mutate(
    Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1),
    Label = case_when(
      str_detect(AIUseStudentsAllowed, regex("unrestricted|anyone", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Yes, unrestricted",
      str_detect(AIUseStudentsAllowed, regex("limited|cases only", ignore_case = TRUE))  ~ "Yes, limited cases only",
      str_detect(AIUseStudentsAllowed, regex("^no$", ignore_case = TRUE))                ~ "No",
      str_detect(AIUseStudentsAllowed, regex("not.*explicit|haven", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Not explicitly addressed",
      TRUE ~ str_trunc(AIUseStudentsAllowed, 30)
    )
  )

ggplot(ai_students, aes(x = Label, y = Percentage, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), vjust = -0.3, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  labs(title = "Students Allowed to Use AI", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```
:::
:::::

::: notes
AI adoption among clinic directors remains limited—only about 13% report using AI in their responsibilities. Student AI use policies are still evolving, with many clinics having no formal policy in place.
:::

## AI Policy Status

```{r}
ai_policy <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(AIPolicy)) %>%
  count(AIPolicy) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

ggplot(ai_policy, aes(x = reorder(AIPolicy, n), y = Percentage, fill = AIPolicy)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 80)) +
  labs(title = "Clinic AI Policy Status", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: callout-important
Most clinics do not yet have a formal AI policy. Many noted this is an upcoming priority.
:::

::: notes
The absence of formal AI policies mirrors broader trends in healthcare and academia. Clinics are navigating this space carefully, balancing innovation with ethical training considerations.
:::

## Routine Outcome Monitoring (ROM)

```{r}
rom_labels <- c(
  "ROMsystem_1" = "OQ-45",
  "ROMsystem_2" = "PHQ-9/GAD-7",
  "ROMsystem_3" = "PCOMS/ORS",
  "ROMsystem_4" = "Standardized Assessment Battery",
  "ROMsystem_5" = "Custom/Clinic-developed",
  "ROMsystem_6" = "Other"
)

rom_summary <- dat %>%
  select(starts_with("ROMsystem_")) %>%
  select(-contains("TEXT")) %>%
  select(any_of(names(rom_labels))) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(!is.na(.) & . != "0" & . != "", na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Count") %>%
  mutate(System = rom_labels[Variable]) %>%
  filter(!is.na(System)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * Count / nrow(dat), 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(Count))

if (nrow(rom_summary) > 0) {
  ggplot(rom_summary, aes(x = reorder(System, Count), y = Count, fill = System)) +
    geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Count, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(rom_summary$Count) * 1.4)) +
    labs(title = "ROM Systems Used (Select All That Apply)", x = NULL, y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
} else {
  cat("ROM system data not available in this dataset.\n")
}
```

::: notes
PHQ-9/GAD-7 and OQ-45 are among the most commonly used outcome monitoring tools. Routine outcome monitoring supports both training quality and client care.
:::

# Social Justice & DEI {background-color="#252b3b"}

## Social Justice & DEI Efforts

```{r}
sj_labels <- c(
  "ClinicSJActivities_1" = "Resources on antiracism – students",
  "ClinicSJActivities_2" = "Resources on antiracism – supervisors",
  "ClinicSJActivities_3" = "New/specialized services for BIPOC clients",
  "ClinicSJActivities_4" = "Outreach to marginalized communities",
  "ClinicSJActivities_5" = "Added DEI didactics/training",
  "ClinicSJActivities_6" = "Significant changes to syllabi",
  "ClinicSJActivities_7" = "New supervisors/instructors for DEI",
  "ClinicSJActivities_8" = "CCTC toolkit on social responsiveness",
  "ClinicSJActivities_9" = "Other"
)

sj_summary <- dat %>%
  select(starts_with("ClinicSJActivities_")) %>%
  select(-contains("TEXT")) %>%
  select(any_of(names(sj_labels))) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(!is.na(.) & . != "0" & . != "", na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Count") %>%
  mutate(Activity = sj_labels[Variable]) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Activity)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * Count / nrow(dat), 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(Count))

ggplot(sj_summary, aes(x = reorder(Activity, Count), y = Count, fill = Activity)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Count, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 4.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = rep(aptc_palette, length.out = nrow(sj_summary))) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(sj_summary$Count) * 1.4)) +
  labs(title = "Social Justice & DEI Activities (Select All That Apply)", x = NULL, y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
DEI-focused didactics and antiracism resources for students are the most commonly reported activities. These data track ongoing commitment to social justice across APTC member clinics.
:::

## Research Activity

```{r}
research_summary <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(ResearchActive)) %>%
  count(ResearchActive) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))

ggplot(research_summary, aes(x = reorder(ResearchActive, n), y = Percentage, fill = ResearchActive)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, " (", Percentage, "%)")), hjust = -0.1, size = 5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 80)) +
  labs(title = "Research Activity in Clinic", x = NULL, y = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: callout-note
Major research themes include clinical outcomes, measurement-based care, telehealth, and training evaluation.
:::

::: notes
About half of directors report being research-active in their clinics. Common themes include clinical outcomes research, ROM/measurement-based care, specialized populations, and implementation science.
:::

# Director Wellbeing {background-color="#252b3b"}

## Burnout Indicators

```{r}
burnout_items <- c(
 "Burnout_1" = "Emotional exhaustion",
 "Burnout_2" = "Depersonalization", 
 "Burnout_3" = "Reduced accomplishment",
 "Burnout_4" = "Work-life conflict",
 "Burnout_5" = "Intent to leave"
)

burnout_summary <- dat %>%
 select(starts_with("Burnout_")) %>%
 pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Item", values_to = "Score") %>%
 filter(!is.na(Score)) %>%
 group_by(Item) %>%
 summarise(N = n(), Mean = mean(Score), SD = sd(Score), .groups = "drop") %>%
 mutate(Label = burnout_items[Item])

ggplot(burnout_summary, aes(x = reorder(Label, Mean), y = Mean, fill = Label)) +
 geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
 geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SD, ymax = Mean + SD), width = 0.2, color = aptc_navy) +
 geom_text(aes(label = round(Mean, 1)), hjust = -0.3, size = 5) +
 coord_flip() +
 scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
 scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 6)) +
 labs(title = "Burnout Indicators",
      subtitle = "Mean scores (error bars = ±1 SD)",
      x = NULL, y = "Mean Score") +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
 theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Burnout scores vary across dimensions. These indicators help us understand the challenges clinic directors face and can inform support initiatives.
:::

## Burnout by Position Type

```{r}
burnout_by_pos <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(PositionType)) %>%
  mutate(
    Burnout_Composite = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("Burnout_")), na.rm = TRUE),
    TenureStatus = case_when(
      is_tt(PositionType)  ~ "TT/Tenured",
      is_ntt(PositionType) ~ "Non-Tenure-Track",
      TRUE ~ "Other/Admin"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Burnout_Composite)) %>%
  group_by(TenureStatus) %>%
  summarise(N = n(), Mean = round(mean(Burnout_Composite), 2), 
            SD = round(sd(Burnout_Composite), 2), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(burnout_by_pos, aes(x = TenureStatus, y = Mean, fill = TenureStatus)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SD, ymax = Mean + SD), width = 0.3, color = aptc_navy) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Mean, 2), "\n(n=", N, ")")), vjust = -0.5, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 6)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = aptc_palette) +
  labs(title = "Composite Burnout by Position Type",
       subtitle = "Mean scores (error bars = ±1 SD) | Scale: 0–6",
       x = NULL, y = "Mean Burnout Score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = aptc_navy))
```

::: notes
Comparing burnout across position types can reveal whether institutional security affects wellbeing. Higher scores indicate greater burnout.
:::

## What Helps & What Challenges Us

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### The Good ✓

-   Mentoring & training students
-   Supportive colleagues & department
-   Making a meaningful impact
-   Autonomy & flexibility in the role
-   Variety & engagement in work
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### The Challenging ✗

-   High workload & administrative burden
-   Lack of institutional support
-   Challenges with students & supervisees
-   Bureaucracy & institutional barriers
-   Pay disparities & lack of recognition
:::
:::::

::: callout-note
These themes reflect open-ended survey responses and are consistent with patterns seen in prior years.
:::

::: notes
The same aspects of the job that directors find positive can also contribute to burnout when poorly supported—particularly supervision and student relationships. Colleagues and institutional support make a substantial difference.
:::

# Qualitative Findings {background-color="#252b3b"}

## Burnout & Satisfaction

![](aptc_2026_presentation_expanded_files/slide_burnout.PNG){width="100%" height="100%"}

## Lessons from the Past

![](aptc_2026_presentation_expanded_files/slide_lessons.PNG){width="100%" height="100%"}

## Supporting New Directors

![](aptc_2026_presentation_expanded_files/slide_newdirectors.PNG){width="100%" height="100%"}

## Best Part of Your Job

![](aptc_2026_presentation_expanded_files/slide_bestpart.PNG){width="100%" height="100%"}

# Summary {background-color="#252b3b"}

## Key Takeaways

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Sample & Position

-   **`r nrow(dat)`** respondents; mostly faculty
-   Median **`r role_stats$Median`** years in role
-   Most hold non-tenure-track positions
-   Majority report to department chair
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Compensation

-   Median salary: **`r dollar(salary_stats$Median)`**
-   Clear progression by rank
-   Most receive PD funding
-   Salary funded mainly by dept/university
:::
:::::

## Key Takeaways (cont.)

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Clinic Operations

-   Median **`r round(client_stats$Median)`** clients/year
-   Therapy + assessment; 89% sliding scale
-   EMR: 92%, telehealth: 94%
-   AI adoption limited (\~13%)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Wellbeing & Mission

-   \~75% support staff; \~67% grad assistants
-   DEI/SJ activities widespread
-   Burnout present; meaning intact
-   Colleagues and autonomy are protective
:::
:::::

## Now to Hear From You: Survey Input

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Was the survey too long?**

-   36% — no, length is fine
-   49% — yes, but necessary
-   11% — yes, shorten it

**Other feedback**

-   Every other year? Little variation year to year
-   Q & A: what questions do you have for us?
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**What do you want to see more of? Less of?**

-   "More on unit (workload assignments) and salary data"
-   "Bilingual service provision (student support, supervision, etc.)"
-   Less "details of how many students are supervised"
-   "You gotta do whatcha gotta do" 😊
:::
:::::

##  {background-color="white"}

::: {style="display:flex; justify-content:center; align-items:center; height:70vh; flex-direction:column; gap:1.5rem; text-align:center;"}
<h2>Now over to you...</h2>

<p style="font-size:1.4em;">

Let's Discuss.

</p>

<p style="font-size:1.2em; font-style:italic;">

Richard will help manage the room!

</p>
:::

## Contact Us {background-color="#252b3b"}

::::: columns
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Jacqueline Hersh, Ph.D.**
Research Committee Co-Chair
[hershjr@appstate.edu](mailto:hershjr@appstate.edu)

&nbsp;

**Ryan L. Farmer, Ph.D.**
Research Committee Co-Chair
[rlfarmer@memphis.edu](mailto:rlfarmer@memphis.edu)

&nbsp;

**Richard LeBeau, Ph.D.**<br>
Research Committee Member
[rlebeau@ucla.edu](mailto:rlebeau@ucla.edu)
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Resources

-   Full analysis report coming soon
-   Data requests: Contact Jacqueline & Ryan
-   [aptc.org](https://aptc.org)
:::
:::::

::: notes
Thank you for your attention. We welcome questions and encourage members to reach out with data requests or suggestions for future surveys.
:::
